subject: Ecology
title: Nectar Rewards
description: A quick win: understand Nectar Rewards.

hook.text: Nectar is the bribe flowers offer—a sugar-rich liquid that fuels pollinator flights.

buildup.text: Plants carefully control nectar volume, concentration, and timing to manage visitor behavior.

discovery.text: Some flowers refill nectar on a schedule, training pollinators to return at predictable times.

twist.text: A few flowers produce caffeinated nectar—it enhances bee memory and makes them return more often.

climax.text: Nectar isn't just sugar. It contains amino acids, lipids, and antimicrobial compounds.

punchline.text: Flowers don't just feed visitors. They drug them.

quiz.question: Why do some flowers produce caffeinated nectar?
options: It enhances bee memory and increases return visits
